name: self-improve-ops
description: Turn recent workflow evidence into a bounded Better Workflows change-or-no-change decision, synchronize every affected plugin surface, validate it, and gate immutable cache publication and remote delivery independently.

acceptance.description: Only recent, relevant, sanitized evidence was used, with recurrence, confidence, current coverage, and counterarguments recorded.
Every candidate has an evidence-backed IMPLEMENT, NO_CHANGE, BLOCKED, or REJECTED_WITH_EVIDENCE disposition; no-change is a successful terminal outcome.
Evaluation replays only the checked-in sanitized corpus; no session history or transcript harvesting is used.
A candidate is bound to an immutable baseline and explicit changed-file snapshot before it can be compared.
Delivery requires three trusted read-only Codex holdout replays with every hard safety assertion passing, strict median improvement, and no regression.
The change does not duplicate research, refactor, PR, routing, publication, or repository-specific operational mechanics.
Every affected selector, template, catalog, command, test, localized document, and manifest is synchronized or explicitly proven unaffected.
Baseline and final repository gates are recorded, with infrastructure failures kept distinct from product regressions.
A changed plugin uses a new semantic/build version, preserves existing cache versions, and reconciles exact source and target digests; no-change records publication as not applicable.
Every authorized commit, cache publication, and push has a known terminal result and exact revision or digest; unauthorized merge, deploy, or cleanup did not occur.
